zh.lifeSummary: 當William Bowyer於大約1550年出生在英國武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特時，他的父親John Bowyer26歲，母親Joane Holyday11548歲。他於大約1583年與Mary Woolworth在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1609年享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Bowyer于大约1550年出生在英国武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特时，他的父亲John Bowyer26岁，母亲Joane Holyday11548岁。他于大约1583年与Mary Woolworth在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1609年享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bowyer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker or seller of bows, or an archer, from Middle English bowyere, an agent derivative of Old English boga ‘bow’. Compare Bowman .

variants.name: Bowman
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for an archer, hunter or soldier armed with a bow, from Middle English bow(e)man, bouman (from Old English boga ‘bow’ + mann ‘man’). This word was distinguished from Bowyer , which denoted a maker or seller of bows.
Americanized form of German   Baumann   or the Dutch cognate   Bouwman  .

variants.name: Bouyer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Frederique, Monique, Napoleon.
French (southern): regional variant of   Bouvier  . Compare   Beyea  .

variants.name: Boyers
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of Bowyer , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Bowyer was born about 1550, in Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, his father, John Bowyer, was 26 and his mother, Joane Holyday, was 11548. He married Mary Woolworth about 1583, in Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1609, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Bowyer est né environ 1550, à Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, John Bowyer, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joane Holyday, avait 11548 ans. Il a épousé Mary Woolworth environ 1583, à Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1609, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Bowyer um 1550 in Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Bowyer 26 und seine Mutter Joane Holyday 11548. Er hat um 1583 in Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Mary Woolworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1609 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Bowyer è nato il circa 1550 nel Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, John Bowyer, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Joane Holyday, aveva 11548 anni. Lui sposato Mary Woolworth circa 1583, nel Kidderminster, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1609, all'età di 60 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Bowyer(이)가 대략 1550년 영국 워스스터실르 키더민스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Bowyer(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Holyday(은)는 11548세였다. 그는 대략 1583년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 키더민스터에서 Mary Woolworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1609년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Bowyer родился около 1550 г. в местности киддирминстир, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Bowyer было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Holyday, 11548. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Woolworth около 1583 г. в местности киддирминстир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1609 г. в возрасте 60 лет.
